results.title: Moonrise timing key for synchronized spawning in Dipsastraea (PNAS)
results.judge_reason: Directly addresses lunar-cued coral mass spawning synchronization mechanism (moonrise timing), strongly matching the source-domain claim.

results.title: Moonlight-darkness dynamics as proximate spawning cues in Acropora (2025)
results.judge_reason: Direct match: explicitly tests post-sunset darkness as a proximate spawning cue in Acropora, the same mechanism claimed in candidate.

results.title: Signaling cascades and importance of moonlight in coral mass spawning (eLife)
results.judge_reason: Covers cryptochrome and signaling cascade mechanism for coral mass spawning, matching the candidate's source-side mechanism precisely.

results.title: Flow-Controlled Scheduling for LLM Inference with Stability Guarantees
results.judge_reason: Addresses LLM inference scheduling but focuses on flow control / stability, not broadcast-synchronized decode emission against a shared clock.

results.title: Continuous batching from first principles (HuggingFace blog)
results.judge_reason: Continuous batching with KV caching and dynamic scheduling overlaps with the LLM target domain but lacks the shared external-clock broadcast framing.

results.title: Dynamic Micro-Batch and Token-Budget Scheduling for IoT Pipeline-Parallel LLM Inference (MDPI)
results.judge_reason: Pipeline-parallel inference micro-batch scheduling is adjacent but does not invoke a global broadcast pulse or synchrony cue analogy.

results.title: FairBatching: Fairness-Aware Batch Formation for LLM Inference
results.judge_reason: Fairness-aware batch formation is an LLM inference scheduling work but unrelated to broadcast-clock synchronization or KV cache reuse cascade.

results.title: Optimal Scheduling Algorithms for LLM Inference (UT Austin, Dec 2025)
results.judge_reason: Throughput-optimal scheduling theory addresses LLM inference scheduling but not via a shared lunar-style broadcast pulse for decode synchronization.

summary.notes: Source-side (coral spawning mechanism) is well-supported; LLM-side scheduling results are relevant context but none match the specific 'broadcast lunar-pulse decode synchronization with cascade KV-reuse' framing, supporting novelty claim.
